title: How to fix a gummy smile with Botox. Gummy Smile Fix
filename: How to fix a gummy smile with Botox. Gummy Smile Fix.en.vtt
excerpt: How to treat a gummy smile with Botox and why you probably shouldn't on this show I'll be talking about a really interesting treatment that sometimes makes people feel happier when they look less happy and sometimes people look less happy when they look happier I'll explaining ho…

segments.text: how to treat a gummy smile with Botox and why you probably shouldn't on this show I'll be talking about a really interesting treatment that sometimes makes people feel happier when they look less happy and sometimes people look less happy when they look happier I'll explaining how this all works as we understand the anatomy the side effects patient selection and why you should and shouldn't treat this area with bot toxin in the show I'll also be sharing a case study I did a couple of weeks ago my thinking behind how I did this particular procedure and how it's affected the patient apparently 25% of the world actually have a gummy smile which is simply defined by your lip being more than 2 millim above the start of your gums and there are actually many different treatments for this in fact some dentists will even shave the gums to make your teeth longer so that you see less gum we can also treat it with dermal fill and in this case with boty toxin so why are patients unhappy about having gummy Smiles the first thing to consider with this is that it is a

spectrum like many things in life and a small gummy smile I don't believe is unattractive I think it goes with a sense of openness and expressiveness that mostly we quite like in faces there comes a point however where it becomes either a distraction to those who are observing the smile or to the person who's smiling so that it stops the patient smiling fully so as soon as a patient is self-conscious enough to try not to smile there is a strong indication to solve that problem aesthetically so that they can simply be in the moment and express themselves more freely so why do some people's lips move up more than others I think this is actually multifactorial and it's not just one answer I think sometimes skeletons are shaped in such a way particularly if people have strong teeth that are projecting forwards that it's almost easier for the lip to fold and slide off the teeth and reveal more of the teeth than in other people and in some it's simply about the strength of the muscle the relative strength of the lip elevators compared with the depressors and the way that the skin and the muscles are attached the geometry around those things that it just lend

itself to pulling too strongly in a superior action but it's never just one thing so interestingly you'll notice that some patients actually develop a gummy smile as they get older so their face becomes more dynamic hyperdynamic in fact as they lose fat that resists the action of the muscle so you'll see a 70 80 or 90y old person do a big smile that pulls their mouth right up in a way that a 25-year-old won't even if it's the same person which would be hard data to get but the principle stands that decre inre facial volume increases facial Dynamics which can increase a gummy smile so that tells you that volume can also be a cure and this is why sometimes dermophil is an appropriate treatment for gummy smile not just relaxing the muscle typically I would choose to use boty toxin in a younger patient with a smaller gummy smile and use filler and Botox in combination with people who are either older or who have a much bigger action caused by their lip elevators it's also important if you're going to treat a gummy smile that the patient has signs of volume loss if you're going to use a

filler in that area so typically this means a nasal Abal fold a shadow that you can then fix that problem and reduce the movement of the lip how common is this treatment well although a gummy smile is pretty common they say 25% of people have one I don't think it's particularly common for patients to ask for it this might be because people get used to it unlike a lot of the triggers for treatment that we have that are triggered by aging a gummy smile is typically something you've always had and it just becomes part of your identity so many patients are not particularly concerned by it sometimes triggers are after a patient gets their teeth fixed they suddenly realize that they still haven't got the smile that they want and it can be appropriate then many dentists will do this as part of a consultation to talk about modulating the top lip and it also often comes up as part of a lip consultation because if you're Consulting someone properly with lip treatments often one of the reasons that they want a lip treatment is to cover their gums because they want to lower it slightly so you can do this with a little bit of filler but only one or 2 mm and then the rest needs to be done with either dampening the smile with filler or boty toxin before you

rush in and do this procedure on everyone with a gummy smile there's something really important that you need to know about it this procedure is unique in one particular way which is that you can create a beautiful result that looks great on a photo that your patient and their friends and family hate why is that it's because the Dynamics of the face change if you change someone's smile it changes a very important part of their communication and sometimes they will look slight odd to people who know them well but not to strangers and that's because we actually know how our friends and Family's faces move when they're being authentic and when that Gap develops between what you know and the change in an important expression it can create a sense of distrust or confusion are you really laughing at that joke or you being sarcastic for example so this is just one of the potential downsides of this treatment the next is it's very easy to overtreat and then you get what many patients will call the cat and the hat appearance so this is this sense of a really long filtrum and a strange smile

that pulls upwards because of over dampening of the lip elevators if you don't know make your patients look like the cat in a hat you need to use very low Doses and learn their faces first and put up with the extra workload of getting them somewhere good rather than just dolloping in a standard dose for everyone where a good percentage of your patients unfortunately will get this side effect or the other one that I just mentioned so let me share with you a case study this is my producer Lila who had no that when she smiles she had a slight gummy smile and we thought we would make a small adjustment and see if she liked it thankfully she seems to love the treatment let's have a look at before and after and I'll talk you through my thinking about how I did this procedure this is a very small adjustment but there are two indications for this treatment one is she has something which I call resting producer face Now Lea is behind the camera here telling me to do an impression of resting producer face but essentially it's this which sometimes makes me feel when I'm being videoed that I'm doing something wrong because she looks either confused or disgusted and that is

one of the indications which is there something we can do just to soften that because actually she's not feeling those emotions most of the time the second one is that when she smiles she simply elevates her lip little bit higher than is ideal and you see more of that of the gum so a proper gummy smile so the two things we thought might be amable to a small dose of Bim toxin now my thinking around Bim toxin for Gummy Smiles is that less is more because I can always add more later whereas you can't take it out and so a small adjustment first time around is better than a fuller dose so I elected to only use two units instead of many practitioners would use three or even four units each side but I feel much comfortable with a two-unit dose and as you can see it's actually worked out perfectly in this case so her Dynamic movement now is still very natural I can't tell that anything shifted but when we look at the pictures we see she has a more technically beautiful smile and then the other thing that's interesting is that her natural resting expressions are still hers but just slightly softer which I think together adds up to an interesting and a

successful result for a very small amount of toxin with two very simple injections it's a lot like treating a nasal abial fold with a bolus like technique where you essentially just slide the needle in until you touch periosteum and do a two-unit injection either side you don't actually have to go as deep as that you can go a little bit less deep but it's a safe place to inject and it does the job reliably well and there are good landmarks there that make it easier than some of the other injections you might see where people inject more laterally or superiorly than that if you found that interesting and you want to learn much more in a much more structured way make sure you click the link below and check out my [Music] membership
